rice.name: Nursery
rice.fertilizer: Apply 0.5% ZnSO₄ foliar spray 15 days after sowing if deficiency appears. Method: Broadcast Carbofuran/Phorate (1.5 kg a.i./ha) 5 days before uprooting.
rice.irrigation: Drain 18-24 hrs after sowing; saturate Day 3-5; 1.5 cm depth after Day 5, then 2.5 cm until uprooting.
rice.pest_watch: Green Leaf Hopper (ETL 1-2/hill);         Gall midge (ETL 1 silver shoot/sq m).                Spray monocrotophos or carbaryl @ 0.5 kg a.i./ha.
rice.disease_risk: Foot rot: Drying seedlings. Treatment: Seed dress with captafol @ 4g/kg or spray carbendazim @ 0.1%.
rice.harvest_signal: Seedlings reach 4-5 leaf stage or required height.
rice.market_signal: Monitor seed and input price trends.

rice.name: Land Prep & Transplanting
rice.fertilizer: Basal: 108.7 kg DAP + 39 kg Urea + 20.8 kg MOP. Incorporate 25 kg ZnSO₄ and 500 kg Gypsum/ha at last ploughing.
rice.irrigation: 2.5 cm over puddle; reduce to shallow 2 cm during transplanting to avoid deep planting.
rice.pest_watch: Stem borer/Gall midge. Seedling root dip in chlorpyriphos (0.02%) for 12 hours before planting.
rice.disease_risk: Soil-borne pathogens. Treatment: Ensure disease-free seedlings; use resistant varieties for BLB.

rice.name: Tillering
rice.fertilizer: Split 2: 81.5 kg Urea + 20.8 kg MOP. Top dress and weed to incorporate.
rice.irrigation: Maintain 2 cm depth. Stress here causes poor root growth and reduced tillering.
rice.pest_watch: Leaf folder (ETL 1 leaf/hill); BPH (ETL 5-10/hill). Spray chlorpyriphos @ 0.5 kg a.i./ha.
rice.disease_risk: Blast: Spindle spots. Spray ediphenphos @ 0.1% or tricyclozole @ 0.06%. Delay N top dressing.

rice.name: Panicle Initiation (PI)
rice.fertilizer: Split 3: 81.5 kg Urea + 20.8 kg MOP + Foliar spray (1% Urea + 2% MAP + 1% KCl).
rice.irrigation: Critical stage; do not let moisture drop below saturation. Maintain thin film.
rice.pest_watch: Stem borer (ETL 5% dead hearts); Leaf folder (ETL 1-2 leaves/hill). Spray monocrotophos @ 0.5 kg a.i./ha.
rice.disease_risk: BLB: Straw lesions. Skip N top dressing; use smaller dose of N in more splits if needed.

rice.name: Booting
rice.fertilizer: Foliar: 2nd spray (1% Urea + 2% MAP + 1% KCl) 10 days after PI spray.
rice.irrigation: Critical stage; keep soil saturated. Avoid inundation >5 cm to prevent root decay.
rice.pest_watch: BPH/WBPH (ETL 5-10/hill). Spray imidacloprid @ 25 g a.i./ha or ethofenprox @ 75 g a.i./ha.
rice.disease_risk: Sheath blight: Grey-green lesions. Apply validamycin 3L @ 2ml/L or hexaconazole @ 0.2%.

rice.name: Heading & Flowering
rice.fertilizer: Final Split: 81.5 kg Urea + 20.8 kg MOP + Biofortification (0.5% ZnSO₄ foliar) at 50% flowering.
rice.irrigation: Critical stage; maintain continuous saturation. Moisture stress causes sterility.
rice.pest_watch: Gundhi bug (ETL 1-2/hill). Dust carbaryl or Ekalux 25-30 kg/ha during afternoon.
rice.disease_risk: Neck blast: Blackened neck node. Spray ediphenphos @ 0.1% or tricyclozole @ 0.06%.

rice.name: Milking
rice.irrigation: Maintain saturation. Do not let field dry to ensure full grain filling.
rice.pest_watch: Gundhi bug. Dust folidol or malathion @ 20 kg/ha. Avoid synthetic pyrethroids.
rice.disease_risk: Glume discolouration. Apply propiconazole 25 EC @ 1.0 ml/L or carbendazim-mancozeb 75 WP @ 1.5 g/L.
rice.harvest_signal: Grains exude milky fluid when squeezed.

rice.name: Dough Stage
rice.fertilizer: Foliar: Final Biofortification (0.5% ZnSO₄ foliar spray).
rice.irrigation: Last irrigation. Provide irrigation 15 days ahead of harvest, then allow to dry.
rice.pest_watch: Rodents (ETL 15 live burrows/ha). Apply bromodiolone (0.005%) baits 6 weeks after transplanting.
rice.disease_risk: False smut: Green spores. Apply chlorothalonil or propiconazole @ 2 ml/L around flowering.
rice.harvest_signal: Grains reach a firm, dough-like consistency.
rice.market_signal: Assess buyer interest for early harvest.

rice.name: Maturity
rice.irrigation: Allow ponded water to disappear. Drain field to prevent root decay and leaf senescence.
rice.pest_watch: Rodents. Repeat baiting if population exceeds working index.
rice.disease_risk: Minimal risk; monitor for grain discolouration.
rice.harvest_signal: 80% of grains in the panicle turn straw-colored.
rice.market_signal: Compare market prices vs storage costs.

rice.name: Harvest
rice.irrigation: Drain field completely to facilitate harvest.
rice.pest_watch: Stem borer. Harvest close to ground level to destroy larvae in stubbles.
rice.harvest_signal: Panicles are fully matured and straw-colored.
rice.market_signal: Sell or store based on MSP and market trends.

groundnut.name: Land Prep & Sowing
groundnut.fertilizer: Basal Dose: 108.7 kg DAP (provides full P and ~19.5 kg N) + 62.5 kg MOP. Incorporate ZnSO₄ (25 kg/ha) and Borax (10 kg/ha) basally. Method: Soil incorporation during last ploughing.
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Pre-sowing or sowing irrigation. Quantity: Ensure soil is at field capacity to facilitate fine tilth and germination.
groundnut.pest_watch: White grubs (ETL: 1 grub/sq m). Recommendation: Apply Carbofuran 3 CG @ 33.3 kg/ha to soil.
groundnut.disease_risk: Root rot/Collar rot: Wilting of seedlings. Treatment: Seed treatment with Carbendazim @ 2g/kg or Trichoderma @ 4g/kg.
groundnut.market_signal: Monitor seed and fertilizer price trends.

groundnut.name: Germination
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Life irrigation. Timing: 4-5 days after sowing. Quantity: Light irrigation to assist seedling emergence.
groundnut.pest_watch: Red hairy caterpillar: ETL 8 egg masses/100m. Recommendation: Set up light traps and bonfires to kill moths.
groundnut.disease_risk: Seedling blight: Patchy emergence. Treatment: Ensure seed treatment was performed prior to sowing.
groundnut.market_signal: Track local labor costs for weeding.

groundnut.name: Vegetative
groundnut.fertilizer: Split 2 (20 DAS): 6.25 kg N (13.6 kg Urea) and 18.75 kg K?O (31.2 kg MOP). Method: Top dressing. Combined nutrient spray (25 DAS): DAP (2.5kg) + Amm. Sulphate (1kg) + Borax (0.5kg).
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: One irrigation. Quantity: Maintain 0.40 IW/CPE ratio to encourage root development.
groundnut.pest_watch: Leaf miner: ETL 1 larva/m row. Recommendation: Spray Methyl demeton 25 EC @ 1000 ml/ha.
groundnut.disease_risk: Bud Necrosis: Transmitted by thrips. Treatment: 2 sprays of antiviral principles from Sorghum/Coconut leaves.

groundnut.name: Flowering
groundnut.fertilizer: Foliar Dose (35 DAS): Foliar spray of TNAU Groundnut Rich @ 5 kg/ha. Method: Spray in 500 liters of water to increase flower retention.
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Two irrigations. Quantity: Maintain 0.60 IW/CPE ratio. Critical stage for yield.
groundnut.pest_watch: Tobacco caterpillar: ETL 8 egg masses/100m. Recommendation: Spray NSKE 5% or Methomyl 40 SP @ 750ml/ha.
groundnut.disease_risk: Early Leaf Spot: Chlorotic spots on leaves. Treatment: Spray Carbendazim @ 500g/ha or Mancozeb @ 1000g/ha.

groundnut.name: Pegging
groundnut.fertilizer: Split 3 (45 DAS): 6.25 kg N (13.6 kg Urea) + 18.75 kg K?O (31.2 kg MOP) + 400 kg Gypsum. Method: Apply Gypsum by side of plants and earth up.
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: One or two irrigations. Quantity: Most critical stage; moisture deficit here severely reduces economic yield.
groundnut.pest_watch: Thrips: ETL 5 insects/terminal. Recommendation: Spray Quinalphos 25 EC @ 1400 ml/ha.
groundnut.disease_risk: Rust: Orange-brown pustules on leaf underside. Treatment: Spray Mancozeb @ 1000g/ha or Chlorothalonil @ 1000g/ha.

groundnut.name: Pod Development
groundnut.fertilizer: Foliar Dose (45 DAS): Second spray of TNAU Groundnut Rich @ 5 kg/ha. Method: Foliar spray to improve pod filling.
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Two to three irrigations depending on soil type. Quantity: Maintain soil moisture to avoid pod shrinkage.
groundnut.pest_watch: Pod borer (Earwig): Damage to developing pods. Recommendation: Apply Carbofuran 3 CG @ 50 kg/ha to soil.
groundnut.disease_risk: Late Leaf Spot: Dark brown spots on leaves. Treatment: Spray Carbendazim @ 500g/ha or Hexaconazole @ 1500ml/ha.

groundnut.name: Maturity
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Regulate irrigation. Quantity: Reduce frequency but ensure soil doesn't harden excessively for harvest.
groundnut.pest_watch: Rodents: ETL 15 live burrows/ha. Recommendation: Bund trimming and baiting with Bromodiolone.
groundnut.disease_risk: Stem rot: Drying of branches. Treatment: Spot drench with Carbendazim @ 1 g/L.
groundnut.harvest_signal: 1. Older leaves dry and fall. 2. Yellowing of top leaves. 3. Inner pod shell turns brownish-black.
groundnut.market_signal: Assess storage vs immediate sale prices.

groundnut.name: Harvest
groundnut.irrigation: Frequency: Final irrigation 3-5 days before harvest if soil is dry. Quantity: Sufficient to facilitate easy pulling of pods.
groundnut.pest_watch: Stem borer. Recommendation: Destroy crop residues after harvest to kill larvae.
groundnut.disease_risk: Aflatoxin Risk: Avoid keeping pulled plants in wet heaps to prevent sprouting and fungal growth.
groundnut.harvest_signal: Pods are fully matured and straw-colored; inner shell is black.
groundnut.market_signal: Sell based on MSP or market trends.

tomato.name: Nursery & Sowing
tomato.fertilizer: Drench with NPK 19:19:19 @ 0.3% (3 g/L) at 18 days after sowing. Seed treatment: T. viride 4g or Carbendazim 2g/kg seed.
tomato.irrigation: Rose can irrigation every day; maintain fine tilth and aeration in raised beds.
tomato.pest_watch: Whitefly (entry prevention); Serpentine leaf miner. Spray 5% NSKE or Endosulfan @ 0.07%.
tomato.disease_risk: Damping off: Drying of seedlings. Drench soil with 0.3% copper oxychloride.
tomato.harvest_signal: Seedlings reach 25 days old with 4-5 leaves.
tomato.market_signal: Monitor seed and hybrid tray costs.

tomato.name: Transplanting
tomato.fertilizer: Basal: FYM @ 25 t/ha + 407.6 kg DAP (75% P dose) + Azospirillum & Phosphobacteria (2 kg/ha). Incorporate into raised beds.
tomato.irrigation: Wet beds using drip system for 8-12 hrs before planting. Maintain paired row spacing.
tomato.pest_watch: Nematodes (ETL: 1-2 larvae/g soil). Apply Carbofuran 3 G @ 1 kg a.i./ha if severe.
tomato.disease_risk: Bacterial wilt: Wilting of plants. Apply bleaching powder @ 15 kg/ha before planting.

tomato.name: Vegetative
tomato.fertilizer: Split 1 (10% N, 5% P, 10% K): 27.2 kg DAP + 32.8 kg Urea + 41.7 kg MOP per ha. Top dress or via fertigation.
tomato.irrigation: Maintain drip irrigation; install laterals at center of beds. Avoid moisture stress for establishment.
tomato.pest_watch: Leaf hopper/Aphids/Thrips. Use yellow sticky traps @ 10/ha to monitor sucking pests.
tomato.disease_risk: Early Blight: Chlorotic lesions. Spray Mancozeb @ 1500-2000 g/ha.
tomato.market_signal: Review production trends for early season.

tomato.name: Flowering
tomato.fertilizer: Split 2 (40% N, 10% P, 40% K): 54.3 kg DAP + 152.6 kg Urea + 166.7 kg MOP per ha. Critical for flower retention.
tomato.irrigation: Critical stage; ensure uniform moisture. Drip irrigation frequency based on soil type.
tomato.pest_watch: Tomato fruit borer (ETL: 2 moths/trap/day). Install 12 pheromone traps/ha.
tomato.disease_risk: Leaf curl: Transmitted by whitefly. Rogue out affected plants; use nylon nets.

tomato.name: Fruit Set
tomato.fertilizer: Split 3 (30% N, 5% P, 30% K): 27.2 kg DAP + 119.8 kg Urea + 125 kg MOP per ha. High nutrient demand for set.
tomato.irrigation: Maintain soil saturation; avoid fluctuation to prevent blossom end rot (Calcium deficiency).
tomato.pest_watch: Fruit borer (ETL: 1 larva/plant). Release T. pretiosum @ 50,000/ha 6 times weekly.
tomato.disease_risk: Mosaic: Virus. Rogue out affected plants and destroy alternate weed hosts.

tomato.name: Fruit Development & Ripening
tomato.fertilizer: Final Split (20% N, 5% P, 20% K): 27.2 kg DAP + 76.3 kg Urea + 83.3 kg MOP per ha.
tomato.irrigation: Regular drip irrigation. Light irrigation reduces late blight risk.
tomato.pest_watch: Fruit borer larvae. Hand collection of larvae from trap crops (marigold).
tomato.disease_risk: Late Blight: Water-soaked lesions. Spray Mancozeb or Copper oxychloride.
tomato.harvest_signal: Fruit changes from dark green to light green or orange/red.

tomato.name: Harvest
tomato.irrigation: Reduce irrigation to enhance fruit quality and transportability [General practice].
tomato.pest_watch: Rodents. Use Bromadiolone CB baits @ 15 g/burrow.
tomato.disease_risk: Bacterial Spot. Spray Streptocycline sulphate on unripened fruits to control.
tomato.harvest_signal: Fruits reach required color maturity (80-100% red for local market).
tomato.market_signal: Sell based on current market price trends.
